definition_note: Historically specific arrangement for organizing production distribution exchange consumption labor property finance and provisioning.
Institutional arrangement through which exchange prices and allocation are coordinated.
Public authority with legal fiscal regulatory and administrative capacity.
Goods or services characterized by non-rivalry and non-excludability or broader collective provision.
Cost or benefit not fully reflected in market prices.
Shared resource or system governed through collective rules and social institutions.
Accumulated productive assets financial claims or ownership claims used in economic activity.
Obligation to make future payments shaping investment consumption fiscal capacity and dependency.
Substantive freedom to be and do things people have reason to value.
Ecosystems resources and life-support functions treated as productive foundations of economic life.
Energy and material flows used and transformed by the economy.
Capacity of an economy to absorb adapt recover and transform under stress.
